skill.name: data-substrate-analysis
skill.description: Analyze fundamental data primitives, type systems, and state management patterns in a codebase. Use when (1) evaluating typing strategies (Pydantic vs TypedDict vs loose dicts), (2) assessing immutability and mutation patterns, (3) understanding serialization approaches, (4) documenting state shape and lifecycle, or (5) comparing data modeling approaches across frameworks.
skill.summary: Assess type models, mutation patterns, serialization choices, and state lifecycle risks in a codebase.
skill.author: Dowwie

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code spans and documentation examples as shell execution, but the file contains no executable script body. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from ordinary prose and template text, so the skill is safe to publish with low residual risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged entries are inline Markdown code, fenced Python examples, tables, and output templates used to guide code review. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution, shell invocation, or user-controlled command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown documentation and examples, not executable skill code. The file has no script block that would run these snippets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Match
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography hits do not reference cryptographic algorithms or operations. They appear to match substrings in normal prose and analysis template text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines discuss data modeling, validation, and output fields. No encryption, hashing, or weak algorithm usage is present.

content.value_statement: Teams often inherit unclear state shapes, weak validation boundaries, and risky mutation patterns. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce a structured review of data primitives, typing choices, serialization, and lifecycle risks.
actual_capabilities: Classifies codebase typing strategies such as Pydantic, dataclasses, TypedDict, NamedTuple, and loose dictionaries.
Identifies mutable state patterns and copy-on-write alternatives in application state handling.
Reviews serialization methods, including Pydantic dumps, dataclass conversion, custom mapping methods, and pickle usage.
Produces a structured data substrate report with core primitives, key files, validation boundaries, and mutation risks.
Connects findings to related architecture reviews such as codebase mapping and comparative framework analysis.
limitations: It provides analysis guidance and does not execute automated static analysis by itself.
It depends on the assistant having access to relevant model, schema, type, and state files.
It may need project-specific context to judge whether mutable state is acceptable.
It does not replace runtime tests for serialization round trips or concurrency behavior.

use_cases.title: Review Agent State Models
use_cases.description: Map the main state objects, validation boundaries, mutation style, and serialization strategy before changing an agent framework.

use_cases.title: Compare Typing Approaches
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether a codebase should use Pydantic, dataclasses, TypedDict, or looser dictionary structures.

use_cases.title: Prepare Refactor Plans
use_cases.description: Find mutable state hotspots and fragile serialization paths before a data model refactor.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Data Model Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when first inspecting a repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this codebase with the data-substrate-analysis skill. Identify the primary data primitives, typing strategy, validation boundaries, and serialization approach.

prompt_templates.title: Mutation Risk Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when state bugs or race conditions are suspected.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use data-substrate-analysis to review mutation patterns in state objects. Highlight in-place updates, copy-on-write usage, concurrency concerns, and safer alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Serialization Strategy Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before changing persistence, APIs, or message formats.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply data-substrate-analysis to evaluate serialization and deserialization. Report methods used, validation behavior, unknown field handling, and round-trip test gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Comparison Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for an advanced design decision across frameworks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a data substrate comparison across the main frameworks or modules. Compare typing strictness, mutability, serialization safety, migration costs, and recommended design changes.

output_examples.input: Review the state layer of an agent framework.
output: Primary approach: Pydantic models for messages and runtime state.
Validation occurs at API boundaries, but internal updates bypass validation.
Main risk: several list fields are mutated in place during execution.
Recommendation: use copy-on-write updates for shared state objects.

output_examples.input: Compare serialization options for a typed service.
output: The service uses explicit JSON conversion for public responses.
Dataclass conversion is used internally and lacks validation on restore.
Pickle appears only in documentation and should not be used for untrusted data.
Add round-trip tests for nested objects and unknown fields.

best_practices: Start with codebase mapping so type, schema, model, and state files are identified first.
Separate confirmed design risks from acceptable local trade-offs in the final report.
Validate serialization findings with tests when persistence or API compatibility matters.
anti_patterns: Do not assume every mutable object is a defect without checking ownership and concurrency.
Do not compare typing strategies without considering validation boundaries and migration cost.
Do not treat documentation examples as proof of runtime behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes type models, core data primitives, mutation patterns, serialization methods, and state lifecycle risks.

faq.question: Does it run code or modify files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is written as analysis guidance and does not include executable automation.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it find concurrency issues?
faq.answer: It can identify state mutation patterns that may create concurrency risk, but runtime testing is still needed.

faq.question: Is it only for Python projects?
faq.answer: The examples focus on Python patterns, but the review structure can guide analysis of similar data model designs.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide access to model, schema, type, state, and serialization code so the assistant can inspect real implementation details.
